stories.headline: Trump defends his Iran deal, criticizes longtime ally Israel as G-7 ends
stories.summary: In a sprawling news conference at the G-7 summit in France, the president touted the economic benefits of the ceasefire and threatened force if it fails.

stories.headline: Death toll in Gaza since ‘ceasefire’ with Israel goes past 1,000
stories.summary: More than 1,000 people in Gaza have been killed since a US-brokered 'ceasefire' between Hamas and Israel was agreed.

stories.headline: US and Iran sign deal as Trump vows to release frozen funds and ease sanctions
stories.summary: President says Tehran will receive incentives when they ‘behave’ and acknowledges it will keep ballistic missiles

stories.headline: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon ahead of anticipated US-Iran framework deal
stories.summary: While violence has declined in Lebanon since a US-Iran agreement to end the Middle East war was announced on Monday, Israeli strikes on the south have killed at least five people since the deal, according to Lebanese state media.

stories.headline: U.S.-Iran Agreement Includes Strait of Hormuz, Lebanon and More: See Full Text of the Deal
stories.summary: New York Times reporters annotated the agreement, which a senior official disclosed on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Trump Angrily Defends Iran Deal and Reveals, Again, His Obsession With Obama
stories.summary: President Trump denied that the United States would be part of a $300 billion rebuilding fund for Iran and argued that his agreement was better than the one Barack Obama struck in 2015.

stories.headline: Tehran says considering plan for Iran, US presidents to sign deal
stories.summary: Trump says he expects the agreement with Iran to be signed 'shortly' without specifying the exact date.

stories.headline: Finland's parliament passes bill in support of lifting total ban on nuclear weapons
stories.summary: Finland dropped decades of military non-alliance to join NATO in April 2023 in response to Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Palestinian residents shocked by devastation in West Bank refugee camp
stories.summary: Palestinian families briefly returned to Tulkarem refugee camp after more than a year of forced displacement.

stories.headline: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite Trump criticism
stories.summary: Speaking on Tuesday, Trump said Israel's PM Benjamin Netanyahu needed "to be more responsible with respect to Lebanon".

stories.headline: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
stories.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

stories.headline: 'It was surreal': British couple describe having warning shots fired near them by Russian warship
stories.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

stories.headline: Warsh Says FOMC Had 'Good Family Fight' on Fed Policy as Rates Stay Steady
stories.summary: Andrew Szczurowski, strategic income portfolio manager at Morgan Stanley Investment Management, said that it is still to be determined if Kevin Warsh will follow through on hawkish signals he gave in his first press conference as Federal Reserve chair as the Fed held rates steady. Szczurowski, who was joined by Bloomberg Economy's Stuart Paul, said that despite the decision to hold rates, the FOMC remains very divided as members disagree on the d

stories.headline: Zelenskyy heads to Brussels buoyed by new G7 commitments
stories.summary: Ukraine secured fresh G7 military and diplomatic backing, including air defence support, as pressure grows on Russia to negotiate.

stories.headline: Bus with Belarus children's football team hit by a drone in Russia, Moscow claims
stories.summary: A bus carrying a children's football team from Belarus in Russia's border Bryansk region was hit by a drone, according to Russian officials. Moscow claims it was attacked by Ukraine, but Kyiv denies it carried out any operations in the area.

stories.headline: UK officials expect Russia to retaliate for seizure of shadow fleet oil tanker
stories.summary: No formal warning issued to captains or ship owners, but industry body says they are exercising greater vigilanceBritish officials believe Russia will try to retaliate for the Royal Marines’ seizure of the oil tanker Smyrtos, prompting UK ship owners to exercise greater vigilance until tensions with Moscow ease.Military sources said the UK had considered possible responses to the seizure of the vessel carrying Russian crude worth $40m (£30m) to I

stories.headline: US Federal Reserve holds rates steady under new chair Warsh
stories.summary: In its projections, the US Fed anticipated that rates will rise a quarter of a percentage point by end of 2026.

stories.headline: Rutte heads to Washington to smooth NATO tensions ahead of July summit
stories.summary: EXCLUSIVE: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte is travelling to Washington next Tuesday ahead of the annual NATO summit in Ankara in July. Relations between the US and its NATO allies have been strained after Trump accused European nations of abandoning the US during its war in Iran.

stories.headline: From regime change to regime deal? How Tehran's political camps are reacting to leaked US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Iran's hardliners have framed the framework deal as a victory, even as Trump dismissed reports of a $300bn reconstruction fund and warned he would resume strikes if Tehran did not comply.

stories.headline: Equatorial Guinea government resigns after failing to meet targets
stories.summary: The vice-president says the government had barely reached 10% of its targets, without specifying them.

stories.headline: US retail sales jump in sign consumers are weathering petrol shock
stories.summary: Data shows economy remains in robust shape as Kevin Warsh takes charge of Fed

stories.headline: New Zealand’s Economy Grew 0.8% Before Iran War Sapped Momentum
stories.summary: New Zealand’s economy sparked into life in the first three months of the year, responding to low interest rates and a lift in spending that largely pre-dated the Iran war.

stories.headline: Warsh Rocks Bond Market in Debut, Sparks Surge in Rate-Hike Bets
stories.summary: It didn’t take Kevin Warsh long to get the bond market’s attention.

stories.headline: Highlights From Fed Chair Warsh’s First News Conference
stories.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh spoke at his first news conference since being appointed the central bank's chair. The Fed's policy-setting Federal Open Market Committee decided to leave rates unchanged and vowed to deliver price stability. Here are some of the key moments from Warsh's news conference. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Crude Oil Edges Lower as Deal to Reopen Hormuz Goes Into Effect
stories.summary: Oil edged lower as traders assessed the interim US-Iran peace deal, with state television in the Islamic Republic and a US official saying that the pact had been signed digitally by the nations’ presidents.

stories.headline: Chef Kwame Onwuachi on New Las Vegas Restaurant, Tatiana
stories.summary: James Beard award wining Chef Kwame Onwuachi, chef of hit restaurants Tatiana, Dōgon, and more, talks about the opening of his new restaurant in Las Vegas, Maroon. He discusses how the infusion of his Afro-Caribbean culture into his restaurants has connected to so many people. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bloomberg Surveillance: The Fed Decides 6/17/2026
stories.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh vowed to restore price stability after officials left interest rates unchanged and signaled growing support for rate hikes this year. Lisa Abramowicz and Scarlet Fu host a special edition of "Bloomberg Surveillance" covering the latest news from the Federal Reserve. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump Says US, Israel Have 'Little Dispute Over Lebanon'
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump, speaking at the Group of Seven leaders meeting in Evian, France, says he and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu "have a little dispute over Lebanon." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: BlackRock's Rosenberg Warns About Overplaying the Flattening Yield Curve
stories.summary: Jeffrey Rosenberg, portfolio manager of the systematic multi-strategy fund at BlackRock, reacts to the Federal Reserve's decision to keep rates unchanged and says there's a risk of overplaying the flattening yield curve. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: After draping White House in gold, Trump heads to ‘the real deal’: Versailles

stories.headline: ‘Send them back’ chants in EU Parliament after anti-migrant bill passes

stories.summary: Donald Trump was welcomed to the Palace of Versailles on Wednesday evening for a private reception and dinner hosted by French President Emmanuel Macron following the conclusion of the G7 summit in Evian.

stories.headline: Europeans leave G7 feeling relieved, balancing self-interest and flattery to keep Trump in check
stories.summary: Macron hailed the G7 as an "objectively successful" summit after leaders agreed on key issues from Ukraine and Iran to critical minerals. European officials point to growing convergence with Washington, but Trump's volatile diplomacy continues to cloud transatlantic relations.

stories.headline: Six takeaways from the G7 summit in Évian
stories.summary: G7 leaders gathered in France for a high-stakes meeting over the last three days to discuss Ukraine, the Middle East and world challenges. Here is what you need to know.

stories.headline: UK minister ‘very confident’ of getting triple EU reset deal done at July summit
stories.summary: Nick Thomas-Symonds tells Euronews he’s “very confident” London and Brussels will close new pacts on agri-food exports, electricity and youth mobility when they convene for a summit in July, as domestic pressure mounts on his government.

stories.headline: Leader of South Africa's second biggest party wants his predecessor sacked as minister
stories.summary: John Steenhuisen is one of South Africa's best-known politicians after leading the DA for seven years.

stories.headline: Bolivia signs $20m deal with US to fight drug trafficking, foreign ministry says
stories.summary: Under its new president, Bolivia is re-establishing severed ties with the US to combat organised crime.

stories.headline: Extraordinary Messi makes more history in masterclass for the ages
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland both enjoyed a fine start to the World Cup - but on a day packed with superstars, Lionel Messi still took top billing.

stories.headline: Pimco Favors Australian Bonds, Betting on Rate Cuts Next Year
stories.summary: Pacific Investment Management Co. is favoring five- to 10-year Australian debt, wagering the central bank will ultimately be forced to pivot to interest rate cuts to support a slowing economy.

stories.headline: BOJ Will Hike Rates Again by December, Says 90% of Economists
stories.summary: An overwhelming majority of Bank of Japan watchers anticipate the central bank will raise interest rates again by the end of this year, following a hike to the highest level since 1995 earlier this week, according to a Bloomberg survey.

stories.headline: Brazilian Nickel Seeks Anchor Investor for $1.4 Billion Mine
stories.summary: Brazilian Nickel Ltd. is seeking an anchor investor to help attract more equity funding for its planned $1.4 billion nickel and cobalt mine in northeastern Brazil.

stories.headline: Cannabis commercialisation not decriminalisation drives up usage, study finds

stories.headline: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event

stories.headline: Drug monopoly extension splits EU ministers in Biotech Act debate
stories.summary: EU health ministers split over a proposal to give certain innovative medicines an extra year of market protection, with EU Health Commissioner arguing the incentive is needed to keep biotech investment in Europe. Incoming Irish presidency, in the meantime, says it will work as quickly as possible.

stories.headline: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
stories.summary: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

stories.headline: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
stories.summary: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.

stories.headline: Fujitsu chair resigns after ‘woman-related inappropriate conduct’

stories.headline: Trump aims to end Iran war but nuclear issue remains unresolved

stories.headline: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.

stories.headline: 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies falling into volcanic crater
stories.summary: Al-Qaqa Ibn Antar had been attempting to climb its walls without safety equipment when he fell in, local authorities say.

stories.headline: South Korea's Starbucks to shut for staff history lesson after backlash
